literal_meaning: from the arising of this, this arises
preferred_translation: from the arising of this, this arises
definition: Companion conditionality formula stressing arising from an antecedent condition.
notes: Use this record when the formula explicitly names the arising of a condition rather than only its presence. It belongs with the four standard conditionality lines. The Buddhadasa usage profile supports treating this line as part of the immediate conditionality formula family.

context_rules.rendering: from the arising of this, this arises

context_rules.context: When smoother English is needed without changing the meaning
context_rules.rendering: with the arising of this, this arises
context_rules.notes: Use this as a controlled explanatory alternate.

example_phrases.translation: from the arising of this, this arises

authority_basis.scope: Supports treating the line as part of the immediate conditionality formula family.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use as the standard arising-focused conditionality line.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not collapse it into the shorter imasmiṃ sati line when the text explicitly marks arising.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents loss of the line's explicit arising emphasis.
